text:
was the father of Fay and Grisha Jaeger, and the grandfather of Zeke Jaeger and Eren Jaeger. He was also the guardian of Zeke after his son was arrested by the authorities.

Appearance

In the year 817, Jaeger was a middle-aged man with mild wrinkles on his forehead and face. He had short brown hair, round eyes, and wore a formal outfit with a tie underneath his clothing.

In the present, he was now elderly with receding gray hair and required a cane to walk.

Personality

Jaeger is a reserved and obedient man who has fully internalized Marley's narrative about the "sins" of the Eldian people. Unlike more rebellious Eldians, he accepts the belief that Eldians are "devils" who must repent for the atrocities committed by their ancestors, even though these events occurred generations before his time. This worldview shapes his entire personality, making him cautious, self-effacing, and unwilling to challenge the authority of Marley. Rather than resisting discrimination, he teaches his family that they must endure it with humility, believing that acceptance of this burden is the only path toward survival and moral redemption.

He is pragmatic and deferential, prioritizing safety over freedom or pride. Jaeger sees no value in resisting Marley's control, viewing any defiance as both futile and dangerous. This attitude influences his interactions with his son, Grisha, as he urges him to keep his head down and avoid trouble instead of questioning the injustice around them, even though it was his own daughter that was killed. His acceptance of Marley's teachings is not rooted in malice but in fear and resignation; he believes that facing harsh truths about Eldian history is the only way to prevent further suffering. This makes him appear strict and even emotionally distant, as his concern for his family’s safety often overrides any display of warmth or encouragement.

Years later, he came to deeply regret his treatment towards his son which drove him down the path that got him banished, to the point where it traumatized and caused him to go through crazed episodes that he required constant supervision from his co-workers. While unknowingly speaking to his grandson Eren, Jaeger broke down as he went on to blame himself in a speech about how his raising of his son inadvertently made him form a movement with dire consequences, and believed that if he had not been hard on him, he would not have been banished.

History

Jaeger was confronted by a soldier who covered up the cause of Fay's death. He demeaned himself in front of the soldier and assured him that he would teach his foolish son, Grisha, about the ills committed by their ancestors once again. He then explained to Grisha the history of their ancestors, trying to make Grisha understand the consequences of stepping out of line.

After his story, Grisha still attempted to reason with his father and that the soldier was lying. However, Jaeger did not listen and forcefully reprimanded his son, further saying devils' blood ran through their bodies and urged him not to cause his parents to meet the same fate as Fay and that all they could do was stay in the internment zone and lead simple, quiet, modest lives.

In the year 832, after raising Grisha into adulthood, he witnessed his arrest by the Marley Public Security and as a consequence became the caretaker of Zeke, his grandson.

Story

Marley arc

In the year 854, over two decades after the arrest of their son, Grisha's now elderly parents lovingly welcome home Zeke, who returns to Liberio after the conclusion of the Marley Mid-East War.

Jaeger approaches a patient named "Kruger" (who unbeknownst to him is Grisha's second son and his grandson Eren) and introduces himself. Having noticed his conversation with Falco Grice, he tells Eren about the issues the family dealt with due to Falco's uncle's membership of the Eldian Restorationists. Noting how things got better for the Grices when Colt and Falco became Warrior candidates, Jaeger asks Kruger to stop asking Falco favors for the family's sake and advises him to reunite with his own family. When Kruger notes on how touchy he seemed about family, Jaeger mentions his children, expressing regrets about what fates befell them due to his methods before breaking down. He is then sent away by his colleagues.

Following Zeke's defection to Paradis Island, Jaeger and his wife are both imprisoned by Marley authorities.

War for Paradis arc

When Eren arrives at Marley's mainland with an army of Colossal Titans, Jaeger and his wife's captors flee for their lives, leaving the two trapped and unable to escape the Rumbling.

Trivia

Jaeger and Dennis Aiblinger share the same Japanese voice actor.
